dataset: Google Earth Pro Satellite Imagery (Giza Plateau, Egypt)

pi_items.source: Matches task description and export_result.sh KML_PATH variable. Live tested: ga user can write to /home/ga/Documents/.
Matches task description. Live tested: ga user can write PNG to /home/ga/Documents/. File > Save > Save Image dialog confirmed accessible.
Live tested: file exists (601 bytes, 9 lines), created by setup_task.sh. Columns: point_id,name,latitude,longitude,type,notes. 8 data rows. File > Import dialog opens with CSV filter accepting *.csv.
Task description specifies this exact name. Add > Folder dialog confirmed accessible in live test.
Task description specifies these exact names. Add > Folder confirmed accessible for subfolder creation.

Latitude 29.9792 matches the widely cited 29.9792458°N (coordinatesfinder.com, Facebook/IFLScience speed-of-light references). Longitude 31.1342 matches coordinatesfinder.com value 31.134094. Live test: navigating to 29.976,31.131 shows pyramid visible in GE satellite imagery.

Online coordinate databases vary (latitude.to gives 29.9723/31.1252 which places pyramid >1km SW of Khufu, clearly inaccurate). Metadata coords produce Khufu-Khafre distance of 475.5m via haversine, consistent with known ~450m separation. Live GE test confirms pyramid visible at these coords, positioned SW of Khufu. Verified via satellite imagery.
Coords produce Khafre-Menkaure distance of 477.4m via haversine. Menkaure is smallest and furthest SW, consistent with known Giza layout. Verified via live GE satellite imagery.
Matches city2map.com coordinates (29.97527, 31.137568) within ~14m. Sphinx is east of pyramids, consistent with known Giza layout. Verified via live GE satellite imagery.

Valley Temple is near the Sphinx (134.8m south per haversine), east of the pyramids. Consistent with known Giza layout (Wikipedia: temple connected to pyramid by 494.6m causeway). Verified via live GE satellite imagery.
Task description specifies this name. Wikipedia confirms the causeway connects the Valley Temple to the Pyramid of Khafre. Add > Path dialog confirmed accessible in live test.

Task description specifies 'at least 5 waypoints'. The causeway is 494.6m long (Wikipedia: Pyramid of Khafre article), so 5 waypoints gives ~100m spacing, which is reasonable for tracing the visible remains.

500m tolerance is appropriate: the pyramids are 475-477m apart (computed), so tolerance allows imprecise placement but still requires being at the correct pyramid. At 30°N, 500m ≈ 0.0045° latitude or 0.0052° longitude.
All 5 placemark coordinates and all 8 CSV data points fall within these bounds (independently computed). Bounds span ~2.2km lat x ~1.9km lon, covering the entire Giza Plateau complex.

Independently computed via haversine formula using metadata coordinates (29.9792,31.1342) to (29.9761,31.1308) = 475.5m. Range 350-550 is valid and contains the computed value. Archaeological sources describe Khafre as 'a few hundred metres to the south-west' (Wikipedia).
Independently computed via haversine formula using metadata coordinates (29.9761,31.1308) to (29.9725,31.1281) = 477.4m. Range 370-570 is valid and contains the computed value.

Task description specifies 2.0x. Live tested: Tools > Options > 3D View dialog shows Elevation Exaggeration field with range 0.01-3. Currently reset to 1.0 by setup_task.sh. Value 2.0 is within valid range.
Tolerance of 0.3 means accepting 1.7-2.3. This is reasonable — the GE Options dialog uses a spinner/text field where the agent might type '2' instead of '2.0', both within tolerance.

privileged_info_summary: The Giza Plateau pyramids are located at: Great Pyramid of Khufu (29.9792°N, 31.1342°E), Pyramid of Khafre (29.9761°N, 31.1308°E), Pyramid of Menkaure (29.9725°N, 31.1281°E). The Great Sphinx is at (29.9753°N, 31.1376°E), and the Valley Temple of Khafre is at (29.9741°N, 31.1378°E), both east of the main pyramids. The straight-line distance from Khufu to Khafre is approximately 476m and from Khafre to Menkaure is approximately 477m (computed via haversine). In Google Earth Pro the Ruler tool should show values in the range of 400-500m for these measurements. The Khafre Causeway is an ancient processional route running 494.6m from the Valley Temple northwest uphill to the Pyramid of Khafre; its remains are visible in satellite imagery as a faint linear feature. The three pyramids are arranged in a rough diagonal line from northeast (Khufu) to southwest (Menkaure). In satellite imagery the pyramids appear as distinct square shapes casting diagonal shadows, adjacent to Cairo's urban sprawl to the east. The CSV data file at /home/ga/Documents/giza_reference_points.csv contains 8 reference points with columns: point_id, name, latitude, longitude, type, notes. Terrain exaggeration should be set to 2.0x (valid range in Options dialog: 0.01-3.0; setup resets it to 1.0). Output files go to /home/ga/Documents/ (giza_plateau_survey.kml and giza_overview.png).
